# Uruguay National Football Team: 2026 World Cup Form, Squad Strength & FIFA Ranking

## 1. FIFA Ranking

- **Current FIFA Ranking: 16th** (as of 11 June 2026, up 1 position)
- **Highest Ever:** 2nd (June 2012)
- **Lowest Ever:** 76th (December 1998)

Uruguay's ranking of 16th places them as the **4th-highest ranked CONMEBOL team** heading into the 2026 World Cup. They were drawn as a **Pot 2 seed** in the World Cup draw.

---

## 2. World Cup Pedigree

Uruguay boasts one of the most storied World Cup histories in football:

- **World Cup Appearances:** 15 (the 2026 edition is their 15th)
- **World Cup Titles:** 2 (1930 — inaugural tournament held in Uruguay; 1950 — the famous "Maracanazo")
- **Copa América Titles:** 15 (joint-most with Argentina)
- **Best Recent World Cup:** Semi-finals in 2010 (South Africa)

---

## 3. CONMEBOL World Cup Qualifying Campaign

- Uruguay qualified for the 2026 World Cup by finishing **4th in the CONMEBOL round-robin qualifying** (confirmed 4 September 2025).
- The CONMEBOL qualifiers featured all 10 South American nations playing an 18-match home-and-away round-robin, with the top 6 qualifying automatically.
- Exact W-D-L record from the qualifying campaign could not be fully extracted, but their **4th-place finish** (behind likely Argentina, Colombia, and Brazil/Ecuador) secured comfortable automatic qualification.

---

## 4. Recent Form (2025–2026)

### 2024 Copa América (USA)
Under Bielsa, Uruguay had a strong tournament run, finishing **4th**:
- **Group C:** Topped the group
  - Uruguay 3–1 Panama
  - Uruguay 5–0 Bolivia
  - USA 0–1 Uruguay
- **Quarter-final:** Uruguay 0–0 Brazil → **Won on penalties**
- **Semi-final:** Uruguay 0–1 Colombia (eliminated)
- **Third-place match:** Canada 2–2 Uruguay (lost on penalties)

### Recent Friendlies / Matches (2025–2026)
- vs Chile — 9 September 2025
- vs Dominican Republic — 10 October 2025, Kuala Lumpur (Won 1–0)
- vs Uzbekistan — 13 October 2025
- vs USA — 18 November 2025
- vs Algeria — 31 March 2026 (friendly)

### 2026 World Cup Group H (Opening Match)
- **Saudi Arabia 1–1 Uruguay** (15 June 2026, Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens)
  - Goals: Al-Amri 41' (KSA), Maximiliano Araujo 80' (URU)
  - Man of the Match: **Federico Valverde** (Uruguay)
  - Attendance: 62,764

---

## 5. 2026 World Cup Group H

Uruguay are drawn in **Group H** alongside:

| Team | Confederation | Pot | FIFA Ranking | WC Pedigree |
|------|--------------|-----|--------------|-------------|
| **Spain** | UEFA | 1 | 1st/2nd | Defending-era powerhouse, 2010 champions |
| **Uruguay** | CONMEBOL | 2 | 16th | 2x champions (1930, 1950) |
| **Saudi Arabia** | AFC | 3 | ~60th | 7th WC appearance |
| **Cape Verde** | CAF | 4 | 67th/68th | World Cup debut |

### Results so far (Matchday 1, 15 June 2026):
- Spain 0–0 Cape Verde (shock result)
- Saudi Arabia 1–1 Uruguay

### Remaining matches:
- Uruguay vs Cape Verde (21 June 2026)
- Spain vs Uruguay (26 June 2026)

**Uruguay's base camp:** Playa del Carmen, Quintana Roo, Mexico (Fairmont Mayakoba hotel / Mayakoba Training Centre).

---

## 6. Head Coach: Marcelo Bielsa

- **Appointed:** 15 May 2023
- **Nationality:** Argentine
- **Notable achievements with Uruguay:**
  - Led Uruguay to "two record-breaking wins against Brazil and Argentina" during his tenure
  - Copa América 2024: 4th place (beat Brazil on penalties in QF)
  - Successfully qualified Uruguay for the 2026 World Cup (4th place in CONMEBOL)
  - Also managed Uruguay U-23 team (2023–2024), including the 2024 CONMEBOL Pre-Olympic Tournament
- **Coaching staff:** Pablo Quiroga, Diego Reyes, Diego Bermúdez (assistants); Enzo Ferrari (GK coach)
- **Tactical style:** Known for intense pressing, high defensive line, and demanding physical fitness; his teams are tactically organized and physically imposing.

---

## 7. Full 2026 World Cup Squad (26 Players)

---

## 8. Key Player Profiles

### Federico Valverde (Midfielder, Real Madrid)
- **Age:** 27 (born 22 July 1998)
- **Vice-captain** of both Uruguay and Real Madrid (2025–26 season)
- Scored his **first career hat-trick** on 11 March 2026 for Real Madrid
- Named Man of the Match in Uruguay's World Cup opener vs Saudi Arabia
- **74 caps** — the team's midfield engine and most important player

### Darwin Núñez (Forward, Al-Hilal)
- **Age:** 26 (born 24 June 1999)
- Moved from Liverpool to **Al-Hilal** (Saudi Pro League) after the 2024–25 season
- **39 caps, 13 goals** for Uruguay — the team's primary striker
- Key concern: playing in Saudi Arabia may reduce competitive sharpness

### Ronald Araújo (Defender, Barcelona)
- **Age:** 27 (born 7 March 1999)
- Extended contract with Barcelona until 2031 (January 2025)
- **27 caps** — elite center-back partnership with Giménez
- History of muscle injuries is a fitness concern

### José María Giménez (Defender, Atlético Madrid) — Captain
- **Age:** 31 — **99 caps**, one of the most experienced defenders in world football
- Long-standing partnership with Godín (retired) now leads the back line

### Manuel Ugarte (Midfielder, Manchester United)
- **Age:** 25 (born 11 April 2001)
- Moved to Manchester United in 2024 from PSG
- **37 caps** — combative defensive midfielder, crucial to Bielsa's pressing system

### Rodrigo Bentancur (Midfielder, Tottenham Hotspur)
- **Age:** 28 (born 25 June 1997)
- Won the **UEFA Europa League** with Tottenham in May 2025
- **75 caps** — experienced midfield presence
- Suffered ACL injury in February 2023 but has fully recovered

### Giorgian de Arrascaeta (Midfielder, Flamengo)
- **Age:** 32 (born 1 June 1994)
- Reached **100 assists** for Flamengo (August 2025)
- **60 caps, 13 goals** — the squad's most creative player
- Scored both goals in Uruguay's 2–0 win over Ghana at the 2022 World Cup

---

## 9. Squad Strength Assessment

**Strengths:**
- **World-class midfield core:** Valverde (Real Madrid), Ugarte (Man Utd), Bentancur (Spurs) — one of the strongest midfield trios at the tournament
- **Elite defensive pairing:** Giménez (Atlético) + Araújo (Barcelona) is among the best CB partnerships at any World Cup
- **Tactical discipline:** Bielsa's system is well-drilled and defensively organized
- **Tournament pedigree:** Deep Copa América 2024 run, including beating Brazil on penalties
- **Depth at full-back:** Olivera (Napoli), Viña, Piquerez, Varela offer quality options

**Weaknesses:**
- **Limited forward depth:** Beyond Núñez, the forward options (Aguirre, Viñas) are not elite-level
- **Núñez's form risk:** Moving to Al-Hilal from Liverpool may have reduced match sharpness
- **Aging creative hub:** De Arrascaeta (32) and Muslera (39, backup GK) are aging
- **FIFA Ranking (16th):** Not among the top-tier favorites, but consistently competitive
- **Key players in non-elite leagues:** Several squad members play in Brazil, Mexico, and Saudi Arabia rather than Europe's top 5 leagues

**Club Distribution:**
- ~5 players in top European clubs (Real Madrid, Barcelona, Atlético Madrid, Napoli, Man Utd, Spurs)
- ~6 players in Brazilian clubs (Flamengo, Internacional, Palmeiras, Fluminense)
- ~4 players in Mexican clubs (Monterrey, América, Tigres)
- ~3 players in Portuguese/Greek/English mid-tier clubs
- 1 player in Saudi Arabia (Núñez), 1 in USA (MLS)

---

## 10. Current World Cup Status

As of **16 June 2026**, Uruguay have played their opening Group H match:
- **Drew 1–1 with Saudi Arabia** — came from behind via Maximiliano Araújo's 80th-minute equalizer
- Next match: **Uruguay vs Cape Verde** (21 June 2026)
- Final group match: **Spain vs Uruguay** (26 June 2026)
- Spain also drew 0–0 with Cape Verde, making Group H wide open after Matchday 1

---

key_facts: Uruguay's current FIFA ranking is 16th (as of 11 June 2026, up 1 position)
Uruguay qualified for the 2026 World Cup by finishing 4th in CONMEBOL qualifying (confirmed 4 September 2025) — their 15th World Cup appearance
Head coach Marcelo Bielsa (appointed 15 May 2023) announced the 26-man World Cup squad on 31 May 2026
Uruguay are in 2026 World Cup Group H with Spain (Pot 1), Saudi Arabia (Pot 3), and Cape Verde (Pot 4, WC debut)
Uruguay drew 1-1 with Saudi Arabia in their opening Group H match on 15 June 2026 (Al-Amri 41' for KSA, M. Araújo 80' for URU; Man of the Match: Valverde)
Spain drew 0-0 with Cape Verde on the same day, leaving Group H wide open
Uruguay's base camp is in Playa del Carmen, Quintana Roo, Mexico (Fairmont Mayakoba)
Federico Valverde (Real Madrid) is vice-captain and the team's key midfielder with 74 caps; scored his first career hat-trick on 11 March 2026
Darwin Núñez (39 caps, 13 goals) moved from Liverpool to Al-Hilal (Saudi Arabia) — the team's primary striker
José María Giménez (Atlético Madrid, 99 caps) is captain; Ronald Araújo (Barcelona, 27 caps) is his CB partner — extended Barcelona contract until 2031 in Jan 2025
Manuel Ugarte (Manchester United, 37 caps) is the key defensive midfielder
Rodrigo Bentancur (Tottenham, 75 caps) won the UEFA Europa League with Spurs in May 2025
Giorgian de Arrascaeta (Flamengo, 60 caps, 13 goals) reached 100 assists for Flamengo in August 2025 — the squad's chief creator
At Copa América 2024, Uruguay finished 4th: topped Group C (3-1 Panama, 5-0 Bolivia, 1-0 USA), beat Brazil on penalties in QF, lost 0-1 to Colombia in SF
Uruguay are 2-time World Cup champions (1930, 1950) and 15-time Copa América champions
Bielsa led Uruguay to 'two record-breaking wins against Brazil and Argentina' during his tenure
Key squad weakness: limited forward depth beyond Núñez; several players in non-top-5 European leagues (Brazil, Mexico, Saudi Arabia)
